title: Austrian Economics for Mortals
subtitle: The common sense behind how the world works
author: Wordsus Team
description: Discover the common sense behind how the world works through 52 weekly lessons on Austrian economics. Learn about subjective value, praxeology, free markets, and financial crises explained simply for everyone.

chapters.title: Human Action: The secret engine of all economics
chapters.description: We explore praxeology, the core pillar of the Austrian School. Understand how real economics is deduced solely from the simple fact that human beings act.

chapters.title: Individual Power: Society doesn't make decisions
chapters.description: We analyze methodological individualism. Discover why only individuals can choose and act, completely debunking the myth of the State as an independent mind.

chapters.title: Subjective Value: Why things cost what they cost
chapters.description: Dive into radical subjectivism. Learn that value does not come from invested labor, but rather from how much a person appreciates something in their life.

chapters.title: Dispersed Wisdom: The great limit of central planners
chapters.description: We study Hayek's dispersed knowledge. Discover why information is fragmented across thousands of minds and no single government can ever centralize it all.

chapters.title: Humans, Not Robots: Why we all make mistakes
chapters.description: We question Homo Economicus. Austrians know we are not perfect calculators, but rather human beings constantly seeking improvement and making mistakes.

chapters.title: Pure Logic: The foolproof method to understand economics
chapters.description: We compare logical deduction against empiricism. Discover why economic laws are discovered through pure reasoning and not merely by looking at statistics.

chapters.title: Invisible Cost: What you lose when making decisions
chapters.description: We delve into subjective opportunity cost. The true price of anything is the enormous psychological value of the alternative you must sacrifice for it.

chapters.title: The Last Unit: The secret to solving market prices
chapters.description: We revive Carl Menger's marginalist revolution. Understand how the final unit of a specific good effectively determines its daily price in the free market.

chapters.title: Water vs Diamonds: Solving the great global paradox
chapters.description: We unravel the paradox of water and diamonds. Learn how marginal utility perfectly explains why a vital good is cheap while a useless luxury is expensive.

chapters.title: Consume Today or Tomorrow: The urgency of human time
chapters.description: We explore time preference. A deep look into human nature and our intrinsic, natural tendency to prefer enjoying things today rather than waiting for tomorrow.

chapters.title: The Language of Markets: Prices speak without words
chapters.description: We analyze the price system as a unique language. Learn how prices instantly transmit crucial global information about scarcity and demand without speaking.

chapters.title: The Fifth Glass of Water: Why we get bored of everything
chapters.description: We explain diminishing marginal utility. Understand why your first glass of water in the desert is pure gold, but the fifth glass loses all of its appeal.

chapters.title: Birth of Money: The great invention that wasn't real
chapters.description: Discover the spontaneous origin of money. We explain how it was never invented by any government, but naturally emerged from barter to facilitate trade.

chapters.title: The Cantillon Effect: Why printing enriches the few
chapters.description: We analyze the Cantillon Effect. Learn how printing new money always enriches the very first to receive it, severely punishing the poorest in society.

chapters.title: True Inflation: The great myth of high market prices
chapters.description: We tackle inflation as a purely monetary phenomenon. We destroy the popular myth that greedy businessmen are somehow responsible for the rising price tags.

chapters.title: The Silent Tax: How the government steals your savings
chapters.description: We expose the terrifying inflation tax. Learn how inflation operates as a completely hidden tribute that secretly transfers your hard-earned wealth to the State.

chapters.title: Banking Illusion: The money they lend isn't actually there
chapters.description: We criticize fractional reserve banking. We expose the fierce debate about how modern banks lend out the money they explicitly promise to keep safe for you.

chapters.title: Safe Money: Why we must demand one hundred percent reserves
chapters.description: We outline the hundred percent reserve ratio. Discover the Austrian proposal to prevent modern commercial banks from creating artificial credit out of nowhere.

chapters.title: Return to Gold: The urgent search for truly sound money
chapters.description: We explore the gold standard and sound money. Why backing the currency with a physical asset successfully limits political power to print endless banknotes.

chapters.title: Private Currencies: The brilliant plan that inspired Bitcoin
chapters.description: We analyze the denationalization of money. Hayek's groundbreaking proposal on private competing currencies that laid the philosophical foundations for crypto.

chapters.title: Crises Explained: Why the world economy always collapses
chapters.description: We introduce the Austrian Business Cycle Theory. Learn that major crises are not capitalist failures, but stem from the blatant manipulation of central banks.

chapters.title: The Price of Time: What the interest rate says about you
chapters.description: We reveal the interest rate as the price of time. Discover that it is not a simple financial invention, but a pure reflection of your natural human impatience.

chapters.title: False Signals: The massive harm of forcing rates down
chapters.description: We explain the manipulation of interest rates. Observe the enormous disasters that unfold when a Central Bank artificially sends false economic signals today.

chapters.title: The Investment Mirage: Building grand castles in the air
chapters.description: We study malinvestment. Discover how cheap credit misleads entrepreneurs into investing massive resources into long-term projects society never really demanded.

chapters.title: The Artificial Party: Why economic booms are an illusion
chapters.description: We explore the dangerous boom phase. We analyze the totally misleading initial euphoria that is artificially generated by the cheap credit from central banks.

chapters.title: The Financial Hangover: The inevitable burst of the crisis
chapters.description: We face the dreaded bust phase. That harsh, unavoidable moment where pure reality imposes itself, the bubble violently bursts, and the market must adjust.

chapters.title: The Capital Web: Magic triangles of the global economy
chapters.description: We unravel the capital structure and Hayekian triangles. Capital is not a homogeneous block, but complex stages deeply connected throughout the passage of time.

chapters.title: The Hidden Engine: Why saving money makes you much richer
chapters.description: We highlight saving as the great engine of growth. Break the consumption myth and understand that only prior savings can truly foster new, advanced technology.

chapters.title: Letting Them Fall: Why rescuing companies ruins everyone
chapters.description: We question state bailouts for massive corporations. Understand why preventing huge companies from going bankrupt only prolongs a terrible economic agony.

chapters.title: The Market in Motion: A dynamic movie, not a simple photo
chapters.description: We observe the market as a discovery process. Dare to view the economy as a constantly evolving, dynamic entity rather than a fixed, static textbook picture.

chapters.title: The Alert Gaze: The true role of the heroic entrepreneur
chapters.description: We highlight the crucial role of the entrepreneur. Discover how their unique capacity to stay alert to new opportunities drives the advancement of all society.

chapters.title: The Market's Prize: Why making money is deeply ethical
chapters.description: We defend the enormous social function of profit. Earning money without deceit is the ultimate prize society awards you for effectively helping other people.

chapters.title: The Power of Failure: Why market losses are very healthy
chapters.description: We explain financial losses as a great cleaning system. They are the strict signals we consumers use to stop bad businesses that carelessly waste resources.

chapters.title: The Real Monopoly: The danger of state protectionism
chapters.description: We compare legal monopolies against natural ones. Learn why the only truly dangerous monopoly is the one strictly protected through exclusive legal privileges.

chapters.title: Creating by Destroying: The paradox of tech progress
chapters.description: Delve into creative destruction. Observe how innovation eliminates old jobs but effectively generates new, highly productive employment in the long run.

chapters.title: The True Boss: Your supreme power when you buy anything
chapters.description: We consecrate the absolute sovereignty of the consumer. Discover how each of your everyday purchases is a direct vote deciding which company lives or dies.

chapters.title: Socialist Chaos: Why central planning always completely fails
chapters.description: We explain the economic calculation debate in socialism. Without private property, real prices don't exist, and any complex economy inevitably collapses.

chapters.title: The Fatal Conceit: The catastrophic error of politicians
chapters.description: We warn against the fatal conceit of modern rulers. It is a false illusion to believe that a select few can plan society better than millions of free people.

chapters.title: Price Ceilings: The foolproof recipe to create shortages
chapters.description: We destroy the dangerous concept of price controls. We analyze how forcing lower prices creates severe shortages, and forcing higher prices creates unemployment.

chapters.title: The Snowball: How one bad law quickly generates a worse one
chapters.description: We describe the fierce dynamics of interventionism. Discover how one state regulation always causes another problem that they then try to cover with another law.

chapters.title: The Broken Window: What we don't see when spending public money
chapters.description: We illustrate the famous broken window fallacy. The need to focus on that economic growth that never occurred because your resources were forcefully diverted.

chapters.title: The Welfare Trap: How endless subsidies tie people down
chapters.description: We harshly criticize the modern welfare state. Learn how maintaining prolonged government subsidies heavily fosters dependence and completely nullifies effort.

chapters.title: Legal Destruction: Why taxes make you significantly poorer
chapters.description: We point to heavy taxes as the outright destruction of capital. Understand that taking money from society prevents wealth accumulation and halts wage growth.

chapters.title: The Spending Myth: Who should build our streets and parks
chapters.description: We question the very existence of essential public goods. Many vital services currently provided by the State can be offered much better by private agents.

chapters.title: The Great Battle: The epic duel between Hayek and John Keynes
chapters.description: We revive the unforgettable debate between Hayek and Keynes. We compare the visions of state interventionism versus the free market during the 20th century.

chapters.title: Aggregate Blindness: What the GDP hides about your reality
chapters.description: We expose the harsh critique of traditional macroeconomics. Concepts like the GDP actively hide what is really happening between real people every single day.

chapters.title: Useless Math: Why real economics is not just simple algebra
chapters.description: We base the rejection of pure mathematical models. Translating human beings into simple algebraic variables always eliminates the key factor of human creativity.

chapters.title: Chicago vs Vienna: Differences between two liberal giants
chapters.description: We differentiate Austrians from the famous Chicago School. We fiercely criticize the excessive use of math and their strong support for the Central Bank figure.

chapters.title: Idle Money: Fully refuting the great liquidity trap myth
chapters.description: We dismantle the false concept of the liquidity trap. We definitively refute the Keynesian theory claiming people hoard money under the mattress for no reason.

chapters.title: The Great Founder: Carl Menger and the start of the revolution
chapters.description: We pay tribute to the great Carl Menger. Learn exactly how this wonderful school began in 1871 by bravely challenging both classical economists and Marxists.

chapters.title: The Intransigent: The life of Mises fighting against everyone
chapters.description: We remember the brilliant Ludwig von Mises. We review the amazing history of the brave man who rebuilt the Austrian School after fleeing the Nazis in Europe.

chapters.title: Spontaneous Order: How society organizes without a boss
chapters.description: We analyze Hayek's theory of spontaneous order. We explore how complex social institutions naturally arise from human action without any intentional grand design.
